name: Fetch Target Sequence
description: Resolves a gene name, local resource name, or raw DNA sequence into a clean DNA string. Use this as the first step whenever a user asks to fetch, retrieve, or look up a gene sequence for CRISPR or any downstream analysis. Supports two target types: 'genomic_locus' (full chromosomal gene region, default) and 'cds' (annotated coding sequence only). Resolution order: (1) raw DNA string — returned as-is; (2) local MCP resource name (e.g. 'ecoli_rpsl', 'pBR322') — read from bundled file; (3) gene name + organism — fetched live from NCBI Entrez using the selected target_type.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: A raw DNA sequence (A/T/G/C only), a local resource name (e.g. 'ecoli_rpsl', 'pBR322'), or a gene name (e.g. 'rpsL', 'lacZ', 'ced-3'). The tool resolves whichever form is provided.

inputs.name: organism
inputs.description: Organism for NCBI gene search. Only used when query is a gene name. Default: 'Escherichia coli'. Examples: 'Homo sapiens', 'Caenorhabditis elegans', 'Danio rerio'.

inputs.name: target_type
inputs.description: Which sequence to fetch for gene-name queries. 'genomic_locus' (default) returns the full chromosomal gene region including introns and flanking sequence — recommended for most CRISPR experiments. 'cds' returns only the annotated coding sequence from the top RefSeq mRNA record — use when targeting transcribed exons specifically.

outputs.description: A dictionary with: sequence (clean uppercase DNA string), source ('raw_input' | 'local_resource' | 'ncbi'), resource (resource key or gene name used), organism (organism queried), target_type ('genomic_locus' | 'cds'), length (int, bp), note (provenance note). NCBI results also include ncbi_gene_id and ncbi_accession. CDS results also include product.
